summary: Recent updates to the Media IPC (Inter-Process Communication) in Firefox focus on improving how the browser handles media playback through various codecs and encoders. Changes to the way the browser manages the **Content Decryption Module (CDM)** — essential for playing DRM-protected content — help ensure that these components live long enough to work efficiently without wasting resources. Additionally, enhancements to the remote encoder and decoder capabilities mean that the browser can now better distinguish between hardware and software options, optimizing performance based on user preferences. Together, these adjustments not only streamline media interactions but also offer a more robust framework for incorporating advanced media features, paving the way for richer online experiences.
